Pyrus pyrifolia is a species of pear tree known for its edible fruit and medicinal properties. The fruit and leaves of Pyrus pyrifolia are rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can aid in the treatment of digestive issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Pyrus pyrifolia include flavonoids like quercetin and kaempferol, phenolic acids like chlorogenic acid and caffeic acid, and volatile oils like limonene and beta-pinene. The fruit of Pyrus pyrifolia is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, while the leaves are often used in traditional Chinese medicine to promote digestion and alleviate symptoms of fever.
